content_of_post_to_comment:
Reddit shows the worst sides of people. People here have such black and white thinking that it seems half the time you are talking to angry teenagers who are unable to have nuanced thoughts and arguments. Everything is so extreme. Every human flaw makes you a terrible person, every relationship issues means it's breakup time, every post about politics ends up creating fights in the comments, every community that gathers people with shared interests is so obsessive about it. People do not seem grounded in reality. I think social media s including Reddit decrease people's humanity.

As a result you get personally attacked, aggressively downvoted, followed into DMs, banned for defending yourself etc.

Reddit is toxic. I wish I could stop using it as a crutch for my loneliness. Reddit is great but only if you come here knowing what you are looking for. You have to use it as a forum, what it truly is by nature. That's not what I've been doing. I've been scrolling for hours every day for years. At some point I even became toxic myself, just like so many Redditors.

That was a rant. But I'll take any tips people will give me to stop this surfing addiction
